ranked_movies.title: Pride and Prejudice
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned across multiple threads (r/romancemovies, r/janeausten, r/ForeverAloneWomen) as a top-tier romance. Praised for its tender romance, witty dialogue, and mature character development, particularly in the 1995 adaptation.

ranked_movies.title: The Proposal
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in multiple posts (r/romancemovies, r/MovieSuggestions) as a fun, modern rom-com with great chemistry between Sandra Bullock and Ryan Reynolds. Often cited as a prime example of the 'powerful woman/ordinary man' trope.

ranked_movies.title: Notting Hill
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently suggested (r/romancemovies, r/MovieSuggestions) as a classic romance. Celebrated for its charming dynamic between a famous actress and an ordinary bookshop owner, and considered the 'gold standard' for its trope.

ranked_movies.title: Crazy Stupid Love
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times (r/MovieSuggestions) as a standout rom-com that is both genuinely funny and a 'really good movie' overall, balancing its comedic and romantic elements well.

ranked_movies.title: When Harry Met Sally
ranked_movies.reason: A frequent suggestion (r/romancemovies, r/MovieSuggestions) for date nights and anniversary viewings. Loved for its iconic scenes, witty banter, and exploration of whether men and women can be just friends.

ranked_movies.title: The Holiday
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested in multiple threads (r/MovieSuggestions, r/romancemovies) as a light-hearted, cozy watch perfect for a date night or a relaxing evening. Praised for its feel-good factor and dual romance storylines.

ranked_movies.title: About Time
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in r/romancemovies as a beloved film, though with a minor caveat about its plot. Generally adored for its unique time-travel romance concept and heartfelt message about life and love.

ranked_movies.title: Long Shot
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in r/romancemovies for the 'powerful woman' trope. Noted for being a surprising and hilarious fresh take on the romance genre with great chemistry between Charlize Theron and Seth Rogen.

ranked_movies.title: Roman Holiday
ranked_movies.reason: Cited in r/romancemovies as a classic example of the 'powerful woman/ordinary man' dynamic. Loved for its timeless story of a princess finding freedom and romance with a journalist.

ranked_movies.title: Serendipity
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested in r/romancemovies as a delightful modern-era favorite. Appreciated for its charming premise of fate-driven love and its excellent soundtrack.

ranked_movies.title: Two Weeks Notice
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in r/romancemovies as a film where the emotionally powerful woman leads the dance. Enjoyed for the chemistry between Sandra Bullock and Hugh Grant and its fun, comedic plot.

ranked_movies.title: The Fall Guy
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested in r/romancemovies as a fun action-romance hybrid where the man does the chasing. Noted for being an underrated and enjoyable watch.

ranked_movies.title: The American President
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in r/romancemovies as an excellent film with a strong romantic plot. Praised for its smart writing and the performances of Michael Douglas and Annette Bening.

ranked_movies.title: While You Were Sleeping
ranked_movies.reason: Cited by users in r/ForeverAloneWomen and r/romancemovies as a comfort watch and a '10/10' film. Loved for its sweet, whimsical premise and Sandra Bullock's performance.

ranked_movies.title: The Shape of Water
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in r/ForeverAloneWomen as a favorite despite its odd premise. Appreciated for its unique, fantastical love story and the relatability of its protagonist.

ranked_movies.title: Downsizing
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed at length in r/movies as a film with a fantastic concept that collapses in its second half. Included for its high visibility in the data, representing the 'disappointing mid-movie collapse' theme.

ranked_movies.title: Hancock
ranked_movies.reason: A central example in the r/movies discussion of films that start strong but collapse midway. Noted for its squandered potential by mashing two good premises into one mediocre film.

ranked_movies.title: In Time
ranked_movies.reason: Another key example from the r/movies thread. Praised for its fantastic sci-fi concept exploring wealth disparity, but criticized for its second-half collapse into a generic Bonnie and Clyde story.

ranked_movies.title: Spectre
ranked_movies.reason: Top comment in the r/movies thread. Noted for its engaging first half and disappointing second half, particularly the underuse of Christoph Waltz as Blofeld.

ranked_movies.title: The Kiss Quotient
ranked_movies.reason: The top requested book-to-movie adaptation in r/RomanceBooks, with a specific fan casting suggestion. Included to represent the strong desire for fresh romance adaptations.
